The ArrayGenerator class — Creating numpy arrays¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class slicer.arraygenerator.ArrayGenerator(object)¶

Public methods¶

ArrayGenerator(missing_value = 1.0E15, data_type = numpy.float64, masked = True):
Parameters:
  • missing_value (float) – value representing missing value
  • data_type – one of numpy’s predefined types to create an array of.
  • masked (Boolean) – should a masked array be created
create(shape,source = None):
Parameters:
  • source – the source array. If this is a netCDF4 variable, this method check if it has a missing_value attribute and uses it if it exists. This argument can be omitted.
  • shape – shape of the array to be created

Returns either a numpy (standard) or a numpy.ma (masked) array with the right shape.

postProcess(array,squeeze = False):
Parameters:
  • array – array is the array returned by the create method.
  • squeeze (Boolean) – if set to True, single-dimensional entries from array are removed.

This method will create the mask if array is a masked array and might squeeze it. It returns a modifed version of array, or array itself if nothing needs to be done.
